items.title: OPR status quo throughout 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia is not expected to increase or reduce its overnight policy rate at 2.75 per cent throughout the year, despite higher energy-related inflation risks. Kenanga Investment Bank Bhd (Kenanga IB) said a hike is unlikely as inflation remains largely cost‑driven and domestic growth still faces external headwinds. The central bank, Kenanga IB said, is expected to prioritise growth support over pre‑emptive tightening.

items.title: Penang schools battered by storm, officials pledge swift repairs and safety for students
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, May 1 — Four schools in Seberang Perai Utara (SPU), including a primary school, were damaged by a storm late yesterday evening. Penang Education director Mohamad Dziauddin Mat Saad said the schools involved were Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Datuk Haji Abdul Kadir and Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Hashim Awang in Kepala Batas, as well as SMK Gemilang and SMK Permatang Tok Jaya in Butterworth. He said the worst-hit school was SMK Datuk Haji Abdul Kadir, where the roof of a block housing five classrooms was torn off by strong winds, causing structural damage to the first-floor section of the building.

items.title: Rafizi to be questioned by MACC over ARM deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli said he has been summoned by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission for questioning over the government's joint venture with chip design firm Arm Holdings. In a statement today, Rafizi said he received a notice at 3pm to appear at the MACC headquarters in Putrajaya on Monday at 10am. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Defence minister defends high‑achieving SPM student, calls for end to online criticism
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 1 — Kota Tinggi Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin has called on the public to cease all forms of criticism and speculation against a high-achieving Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) student, following the circulation of a video on social media recently. He said the footage showed a casual interaction between him and students during an event in the Kota Tinggi parliamentary and Sedili state constituencies honouring prospective haj pilgrims and 2025 SPM high-achieving students. According to him, the interaction was misinterpreted by certain parties, leading to various negative reactions, including criticism directed at the student.

items.title: Commando's family demands findings of army probe into alleged assault
items.summary: Suzana Norlihan Alias, the lawyer representing the family, said they have yet to be informed of the results despite public statements on the matter. She said the family is seeking full details, including the identity of the investigators and the specific findings. Suzana said the family wants those allegedly involved to face "serious" charges, given Abdul Hamid's severe injuries.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain forecast across almost all states until 6pm: MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nearly all states nationwide are forecast to experience thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds until 6pm today. In Perak, the areas involved are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim. The same warning has also been issued for Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak and Petaling in Selangor, as well as the entire Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Activists on Gaza aid flotilla detained by Israel disembark in Crete
items.summary: ATHENS: Dozens of activists on a Gaza-bound aid flotilla which was intercepted by Israeli forces in international waters off Crete disembarked on Friday in the Greek island, an AFP journalist saw. Israel's foreign ministry earlier said around 175 activists had been taken off more than 20 boats on Thursday. Flotilla organisers put the number at 211.

items.title: Heavy rain helps extinguish week-long peat fire in Dungun
items.summary: Dungun Fire and Rescue Station chief Deputy Fire Superintendent II Yahaya Hanafi, said 3 heavy rain spells from 7am helped extinguish the remaining 1.5ha still burning. He said the prolonged hot and dry weather without rainfall had caused a 34-hectare peat forest area at the site to catch fire since April 24. Hanafi said the peat forest fire had forced the suspension of leave for all personnel.

items.title: Foreign woman dies in suspected fall from apartment in Nilai
items.summary: NILAI: A foreign woman in her 20s died after she is believed to have fallen from the 13th floor of an apartment building in Bandar Baru Nilai at 1.51am today. Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Ahmad Dzaffir Mohd Yussof said the Nilai district police headquarters received information on the incident while a police team was conducting a narcotics raid at the apartment building. He advised the public not to speculate on the matter, as it could disrupt police investigations, and urged those with information to contact the Nilai district police headquarters or the nearest police station.

items.title: Safety of 175 Gaza flotilla activists, including 10 Malaysians, still unconfirmed 38 hours after Israeli detention
items.summary: BANGI, May 1 — The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) has yet to receive any official confirmation on the safety status of 175 Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 activists, including 10 Malaysian participants, after being detained by Israeli authorities in international waters 38 hours ago. SNCC director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said while there was initial information that all the activists would be released at Lerapetra Port, Crete, Greece at noon today (Malaysia time), it has yet to be verified. Sani Araby said they have also been in contact with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim who has stated his firm stand and condemned the Israeli regime for violating international laws.

items.title: AEON to open KL Midtown mall in Q4
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Aeon Co (M) Bhd is set to strengthen its foothold in Malaysia's retail sector with the opening of Aeon Mall KL Midtown, scheduled for the fourth quarter of this year. The new mall forms part of the 75-acre KL Metropolis mixed development, marking Aeon's strategic expansion into the heart of the capital. Aeon Mall KL Midtown will be the company's 28th mall in Malaysia.

items.title: GOF seizes frozen food, aquatic animals worth RM23k in Rantau Panjang
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The General Operations Force (GOF) seized various types of frozen food, as well as fish, prawns and ornamental turtles suspected to have been smuggled from a neighbouring country, worth RM22,760 in an operation at the illegal Lan Bundle base near Rantau Panjang, Pasir Mas, yesterday. GOF Southeast Brigade commander Datuk Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure was carried out by the Strike Force/Tiger Platoon of Battalion 9 under Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at 6.30pm. He said among those seized were 120 rasbora, 20 koi, 60 killifish, 45 nilem fish, as well as various species of prawns and 60 turtles.

items.title: Prime Minister Anwar calls for restraint, stresses economy over politics in Negeri Sembilan crisis
items.summary: RAWANG, May 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged all parties to remain calm in addressing the situation in Negeri Sembilan and to prioritise efforts to revive the economy and safeguard the interests of the people. He said the situation in the state must be handled in accordance with the provisions of the state constitution, while respecting the decree of the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun to continue in office. On Monday, all 14 Umno and Barisan Nasional assemblymen in Negeri Sembilan announced they had lost confidence in and withdrawn support from the state government led by Aminuddin.

items.title: Foreign woman dies after falling from 13th‑floor apartment during police narcotics raid in Nilai
items.summary: NILAI, May 1 — A foreign woman in her 20s died after she was believed to have fallen from the 13th floor of an apartment building in Bandar Baru Nilai here at 1.51am today. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the Nilai district police headquarters confirmed receiving information about the death that happened while a police team was conducting a narcotics raid at the apartment building. He advised the public to not speculate on the matter as it could disrupt police investigations and urged those with information on the death to assist investigations by contacting the Nilai district police headquarters or the nearest police station.

items.title: Negri Sembilan ruler performs prayers at mosque in Jempol
items.summary: JEMPOL: Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, today made his first public appearance following a political crisis in the state when His Royal Highness arrived to perform Friday prayers at the Tuanku Muhriz Mosque in Bandar Seri Jempol. Accompanying him were his two sons, the Tunku Besar Seri Menanti, Tunku Ali Redhauddin Tuanku Muhriz, and Tunku Zain Al-'Abidin Tuanku Muhriz. On Wednesday, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun sought an audience with Tuanku Muhriz following a state leadership crisis.

items.title: Heavy rain and strong winds forecast nationwide, MetMalaysia issues sweeping alert until evening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit nearly all states across the country until 6.00pm this evening. In Perak, the areas involved are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim. The same warning has also been issued for Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak and Petaling in Selangor, as well as the entire Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: No official word on abducted GSF Malaysian activists 38 hours on, says command centre
items.summary: BANDAR BARU BANGI: The Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) has yet to receive any information on the fate of Malaysians allegedly abducted by Israeli forces while taking part in the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) humanitarian mission. SNCC director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby confirmed that 38 hours had passed without any official information reaching the centre. In a related development, Sani Araby said SNCC had received reports that all 175 GSF 2.0 activists were seen at the port of Larapetra in Greece at noon (Malaysian time) today, but stressed that the information could not be verified.

items.title: IOI Properties rides Singapore office rebound
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IOI Properties Group Bhd is emerging as a key beneficiary of the strengthening Singapore office market, with analysts highlighting its growing exposure to prime assets as a major driver of value creation. According to UBS Global Research, IOI Properties is set to deliver sustained net asset value (NAV) growth of more than two per cent per annum (p.a) with the rising Singapore office market. "At around half of total assets and near 66 per cent of IOI Properties' NAV, we expect sustained five to 10 per cent rent reversions on 20- 33 per cent p.a renewals to drive meaningful more than two per cent p.a.

items.title: Blossom Wong, feared Special Branch undercover officer, dies at 87
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Blossom Wong, one of Malaysia’s best-known Special Branch officers and undercover operatives, has died at the age of 87, New Straits Times reported. According to New Straits Times , Wong was regarded as a quiet but formidable figure in the police force. Dr Christina told New Straits Times that her mother’s service in the force never affected their bond.

items.title: Negri Umno wings criticise order to vacate Undang Luak Sungei Ujong's residence
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Umno's three wings have spoken out against the state government's purported order for the immediate vacation of the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong official residence and the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong. "It is clear that the order was issued at a time when the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong is managing an unresolved dispute involving the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong. Yesterday, it was reported that the Negri Sembilan State Secretary's Office issued a letter directing the immediate vacation of the Undang Luak Sungei Ujong official residence and the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong.

items.title: Sabah allocates RM559.85mil to boost food security, agriculture
items.summary: PAPAR: The Sabah government has allocated RM559.85 million this year to strengthen food security and improve the state's self-sufficiency rate. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the allocation to the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry would support various initiatives, including the development of padi cultivation in districts such as Papar, Membakut, Beaufort and Kuala Penyu. Hajiji said the move reflects the state's commitment to strengthening food security amid global economic and geopolitical uncertainties.

items.title: Japanese man arrested for allegedly incinerating wife's body at zoo
items.summary: TOKYO: Japanese police arrested a man for allegedly incinerating his dead wife at the zoo where he worked, officials and local media said Friday, following the discovery of human remains. The victim, 33-year-old Yui Suzuki, was identified by local media as his wife. Police have not said how she died.

items.title: Labour Day: Govt rolls out RM710mil Pace package, maternity aid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: In conjunction with Labour Day 2026, themed "Pekerja Madani, Kesuma Bangsa", workers in Malaysia are set to benefit from several initiatives, announcements and opportunities introduced at the national-level celebration. Economic resilience package (Pace) worth more than RM710 million. - Pace forms part of the government's economic resilience strategy, with positive implications for human capital development and labour market stability.

items.title: "Peace has become a dirty word" as activists rally in Tel Aviv for two-state solution
items.summary: TEL AVIV: Colourful images of doves decorated the backdrop of the stage where the Jerusalem Youth Choir singing in Hebrew and Arabic, opening the main event of the "People's Peace Summit." As one speaker said on stage: "Peace has become a dirty word." Nini, 56, one of Israel's best-known singers internationally and a prominent face of the country's peace movement, performs under the stage name Noa.

items.title: Japan extends Petronas urea deal amid supply fears
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Japan will extend its urea procurement contract with national oil company Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) amid concerns over potential disruptions to global fertiliser supply due to ongoing tensions in the Middle East. According to a news report by Japan's public broadcaster NHK, the agreement comes as the country seeks to secure a stable supply of urea, a key raw material used in fertiliser production. Japan's agriculture, forestry and fisheries minister Norikazu Suzuki said the country currently sources about three-quarters of its urea imports from Malaysia.

items.title: Yayasan Peneraju taps 2,000 Bumiputera youths for accounting pathways
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Yayasan Peneraju has expanded its nationwide outreach to groom future accountants, reaching more than 2,000 Bumiputera youths through its 2026 '"Jom Explore! programme. Yayasan Peneraju said eligible participants were introduced to accelerated pathways to pursue professional certifications such as the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants and the Institute of Chartered Accountants in England and Wales.

items.title: Zahid: Entertainment must respect values, sensitivities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said that any public programme must respect religious sensitivities, cultural values and local norms. "In any programme, the principles must be clear. It cannot sideline values, etiquette, religious sensitivities and local societal norms," he said in a statement.

items.title: Workers welcome post-maternity leave allowance, say it eases burden on new mothers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The introduction of the Post-Maternity Leave Allowance, announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, reflects the government’s concern for working mothers in addressing the rising cost of living. Most public and private sector employees interviewed by Bernama said the additional one-month assistance would allow mothers who have just given birth to undergo their confinement period with greater peace of mind, without financial pressure. Senior assistant director at the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma), Nurfarehin Abd Aziz, 35, said the initiative reflects the government’s commitment to empowering women.

items.title: Penang extends quit rent appeal period to May 31
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang state government has extended the appeal period for cases arising from the implementation of the revised quit rent (land tax) until May 31, said Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. Chow said the decision was made at the State Executive Council meeting on April 29, which agreed to extend the appeal period for cases linked to the revised land tax rates. The exemption will cover late fees for land tax, parcel tax and temporary block tax," he said in a statement today.

items.title: CelcomDigi restores data services after upgrade disruption
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CelcomDigi, the country's largest mobile service operator, experienced an interruption to data services due to a systems upgrade yesterday. The telecommunications giant said it had fully restored mobile data services for affected subscribers. "We confirm that some CelcomDigi customers experienced a brief interruption to data services last evening.

items.title: From Bedrock to Johor: Sultan Ibrahim meets grandson of ‘Flintstones’ co‑creator
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 1 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today received a visit from Dave Williams, the grandson of William Hanna, co-creator of The Flintstones , The Jetsons and numerous other cartoon characters. According to a post on the Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, the meeting took place at Istana Pasir Pelangi here. Williams, who is an economic counsellor at the United States Embassy in Malaysia, also visited Istana Flintstones in Mersing yesterday.

items.title: Disabled man in despair as RM5k loan from 'ah long' turns into debt nightmare
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A disabled man who borrowed RM5,000 from illegal moneylenders to settle a debt now faces a staggering 280 per cent interest rate, causing his repayments to skyrocket. The victim, identified only as Ashraf, 29, said his troubles began when he sought funds to repay a RM2,400 debt to a friend. This burden made me doubt the legitimacy of the loan," he said.

items.title: Food security drive gains RM559.85m boost as Hajiji ties Kaamatan spirit to harvest resilience
items.summary: PAPAR, May 1 — The Sabah state government has allocated a total of RM559.85 million to the state’s Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry (MAFFI) this year to improve food security and the self-sufficiency rate (SSR). Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said various efforts have also been and are being implemented by the government to ensure food security in the state, including the development of padi fields throughout the state, including in Papar and surrounding districts such as Membakut, Beaufort and Kuala Penyu. “In line with the spirit of the Kaamatan Festival which values the harvest, the agriculture sector remains one of the three main pillars that drive the state’s economic growth,” he said at the launching of Sabah state-level Kaamatan Festival here today.

items.title: Thunderstorm alert issued across multiple states until 4pm
items.summary: MET0105 Thunderstorm alert issued across multiple states until 4pm Caption MetMalaysia warns of heavy rain and strong winds in short-term advisory. – Source: Malaysian Meteorological Dept New Straits Times KUALA LUMPUR: The Meteorological Department has issued a thunderstorm warning for several states, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until 4pm today. The alert, issued at 1.15pm, covers parts of Kedah, Perak, Terengganu, Pahang, Selangor, Negri Sembilan, Melaka and Johor, as well as the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya.

items.title: UK police charge man with stabbing attack on two Jewish Londoners
items.summary: LONDON: UK police said on Friday they had charged a 45-year-old man over the stabbing of two Jewish men in north London, the latest in a string of hate attacks. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed Thursday to boost security for the Jewish community after accusations from angry British Jews that his government has repeatedly failed to protect them. The two men were attacked in broad daylight in Golders Green, a north London area with a large Jewish population.

items.title: Foreigners begging at KLIA arrested after videos go viral
items.summary: SEPANG: Two foreign men were arrested at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) today for allegedly begging for money to cover their daily expenses. The suspects, a 28-year-old Tajikistani and a 45-year-old Palestinian, are United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cardholders. Klia police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Ravi said the arrests followed a complaint from Malaysia Airports Aviation Security (Avsec).

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Malaysia working to free detained Global Sumud activists
items.summary: RAWANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia is working with other countries to secure the release of Global Sumud Flotilla activists detained by Israel. Anwar said he had been in touch with the Foreign Ministry and would continue contacting counterparts in other countries to help secure their release. Anwar said many of the activists were detained in international waters, describing Israel's actions as a "mad act" that disregards international principles.

items.title: Blossom Wong, ex-Special Branch and undercover operative, dies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Blossom Wong, one of Malaysia's most well-known Special Branch officers and undercover operatives, has died. Her death was confirmed by her daughter, Dr Christina Blossom Welch who recieved the news around 9am. Dr Christina said her mother's time in the force never weakened the bond she had with her children.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan Umno wings push back, call state’s Balai Undang Luak directive unfair and heavy‑handed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The Negeri Sembilan Umno Wanita, Youth and Puteri wings have expressed concern over the relocation of staff from the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong and the vacating of its official residence, describing the move as insensitive in light of an ongoing customary dispute. In a joint statement, the three wings said they took a “serious view” of the directive issued to relocate all residents at the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong, followed by an instruction to vacate both the official residence and the premises on April 29, 2026. “It is clear that this directive was issued while the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong was managing an unresolved dispute involving YTM Datuk Klana Petra Undang Luak Sungei Ujong.

items.title: Iran activates air defences as Trump faces congressional deadline
items.summary: TEHERAN: Teheran's air defences were activated to counter small aircraft and drones late Thursday, as the White House signalled that it will not be reined in by a congressional deadline on the Iran war. US President Donald Trump's administration faced a looming midnight deadline to secure congressional authorisation for the war against Iran, setting up a clash between the White House and Congress. 'SHAMEFUL DEFEAT' Earlier on Thursday, Iran's supreme leader declared that the United States had suffered a shameful defeat, defiantly rejecting a warning from Trump that an economically punishing US naval blockade could be enforced for months to come.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar urges calm in Negri Sembilan political crisis
items.summary: RAWANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged all parties in Negri Sembilan to allow the state's political situation to cool down, saying stability is crucial to avoid disrupting efforts to restore the economy and safeguard public interests amid global uncertainties. He said the matter must be handled in accordance with the state constitution and relevant laws. "Negri Sembilan, let the situation cool down first.

items.title: Anwar says no more waiting, workers’ initiatives must hit ministers’ desks in two weeks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has given all relevant agencies a two-week deadline to submit reports on the implementation of each workers’ initiative announced today. Today, the announcement is made; within two weeks the report must be on the minister’s desk, and must be presented for Cabinet consideration within three weeks,” he said. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Human Resource Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Cabinet members, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, senior government officials, as well as representatives of workers’ unions and employers.

items.title: Penang picked for Indian Ocean maritime heritage Unesco bid involving 17 countries
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 1 — Penang has been selected to participate in the Indian government’s initiative for a transnational Indian Ocean maritime Unesco listing. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the initiative involves 17 countries around the Indian Ocean, including Malaysia. He said the maritime aspect of the Indian Ocean is not the sole prerogative of the Indian subcontinent.

items.title: Chow Kon Yeow announces late fee exemptions as Penang land tax review sparks over 10,000 appeals
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 1 — The Penang State Government has extended the appeal period for cases arising from the implementation of the new quit rent (land tax) review until May 31, said Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. Chow said the decision was made through the State Executive Council (MMK) meeting dated April 29, which agreed to extend the appeal period for cases arising from the implementation of the new land tax review until May 31. He said the state government hoped that with the exemption from land tax, parcel tax and temporary block tax after May 31, it would give all landowners the opportunity to pay their land tax as soon as possible and avoid arrears on their tax accounts by 2027.

items.title: Fuel subsidies keep lid on transport costs, but Tengku Zafrul warns supply crunch will test households
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The impact of the global supply crisis on the cost of living is expected to begin to be felt by Malaysians within one to two months, said senior political advisor to the Prime Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz. He said this was driven by increased costs in the supply chain including logistics, fertiliser and other industrial inputs. Tengku Zafrul said among the sectors expected to be affected first are logistics, followed by agriculture and industries that rely on raw materials such as plastic.

items.title: Anwar pushes for fairer wages, telling corporate Malaysia to match profits with compassion for workers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on employers to be more considerate and compassionate in ensuring the welfare of workers continues to be protected. He said peace and rapid national development must be matched by guarantees of well-being for all segments of society, including workers. That is the priority,” he said at the national-level 2026 Workers’ Day celebration here today.

items.title: Average formal sector wage rises to RM3,167 in December 2025, says DOSM
items.summary: In comparison, female employees, who make up 44.9 per cent (3.18 million), recorded a median wage of RM3,120. In terms of ethnicity, Bumiputera formal employees recorded a median monthly wage of RM2,627, while Chinese employees earned RM4,767. Indian employees recorded a median wage of RM3,120 in December 2025.

items.title: Anwar rolls out allowance plan, giving mothers 80pc salary support for longer recovery after childbirth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The government will introduce the Post-Maternity Leave Allowance as a progressive measure to support women’s participation in the workforce and reduce the stress faced by mothers after giving birth. He said the allowance would be introduced through an amendment to the Employment Insurance System Act 2017 and is aimed at providing additional financial support to mothers who needed longer time after maternity leave. Anwar said the initiative is expected to benefit more than 132,000 female workers nationwide.

items.title: HKL says ‘war zone’ viral claim due to ongoing emergency department upgrade works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR — Hospital Kuala Lumpur (HKL) has clarified that images circulating on social media depicting its Emergency and Trauma Department as a “war zone” since Wednesday are due to ongoing upgrading works. HKL director Datuk Dr Harikrishna K R Nair said the project is a strategic plan to enhance patient capacity and improve service comfort. “Despite space constraints, healthcare services at the Emergency and Trauma Department continue as usual under the ‘no wrong door’ principle,” he said in a statement.

items.title: JPJ denies viral claims of free driving licence offer, warns public of fake ads
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 1 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has denied claims circulating on social media, including Threads, alleging that free driving licences are being offered. JPJ stressed that the advertisements are false and invalid. “The public is encouraged to always verify information through official JPJ channels before believing or sharing any information.

items.title: Anwar announces RM710m PACE package to strengthen jobs and human capital development
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced the Progressive Acceleration for Capability and Employment (PACE) Economic Resilience Package, valued at more than RM710 million, as part of efforts to strengthen human capital development and ensure the stability of the national labour market. The Prime Minister said PACE forms part of the country’s economic resilience strategy. He also announced that the Self-Employment Social Security Act 2017 will be amended to expand protection under the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) to Malaysians working abroad, beginning with cross-border workers between Malaysia and Singapore.

items.title: Global economic conditions more challenging now than in February, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The government will continue to take the necessary measures in facing global economic uncertainty, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the global economic situation has become more challenging this month compared to three months ago in February. He said this when delivering his Workers’ Day message in conjunction with the National Workers’ Day 2026 celebration held here today.

items.title: Zambry: Education gaps start early, calls for holistic overhaul of Bumiputera education system
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir said educational gaps begin long before students enter university, calling for a holistic overhaul of Bumiputera education. He added that Bumiputera education must be approached in a comprehensive manner, spanning early childhood education, formal schooling, skills training, higher education, and a culture of lifelong learning. Among the key areas of focus are the review of Bumiputera education policies, mainstreaming Tahfiz education, strengthening Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET), early childhood education, education endowment, and the future direction of national education.

items.title: Tourism minister urges critics to stop politicising Rain Rave festival, says it boosts economy and supports traders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing is calling on all parties to stop politicising the Rain Rave Water Music Festival (RRWMF), which is currently taking place in Bukit Bintang. He said the programme aims to help spur the local economy while also giving everyone a chance to enjoy their Labour Day holiday. Tiong reiterated that RRWMF is not a Songkran festival, but a water-themed music festival, and reassured the public that visitor safety remains the top priority.

items.title: Storm damages over 30 homes and premises in Kepala Batas, no casualties reported
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, April 30 — More than 30 homes and business premises around Kepala Batas were reported damaged following a storm and strong winds this evening. John Sagun said the Kepala Batas Fire and Rescue Station was also affected when its roof was blown off by strong winds. Apart from Kepala Batas Hospital, similar incidents were also reported in several other areas around Penaga and Kepala Batas,” he said.

items.title: Over 15,000 expected at National Workers’ Day 2026 celebration in Bukit Jalil today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — More than 15,000 workers from the public and private sectors nationwide are expected to gather at the National Workers’ Day 2026 celebration at Unifi Arena, Bukit Jalil today. The celebration, themed “Pekerja Madani Kesuma Bangsa”, will be officiated by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also scheduled to deliver a special address. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan is also expected to attend.

items.title: Tiong says Malaysia’s tourist arrivals continue to grow despite Middle East conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said tourist arrivals in Malaysia continue to grow despite the Middle East conflict. Tiong said this during a press conference with local media following the launch of the Rain Rave Water Music Festival (RRWMF) at Bukit Bintang last night. “Up until today, our tourist numbers continue to grow, thank God for helping us.

items.title: Police arrest four foreigners in Seremban over alleged obscene video scam syndicate
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 1 — Police detained four Chinese nationals during a raid here on April 23 for suspected involvement in an online scam syndicate selling obscene videos. Seremban Deputy Police Chief Supt Ahmad Lutfti Abdul Mutalip said the suspects, aged between 25 and 40, were apprehended by a team from the District Police Headquarters (IPD) Commercial Crime Investigation Division and the Negeri Sembilan Contingent Police Headquarters Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID). He said police seized four computers, 19 mobile phones of various brands, a router, a set of keys, and a remote control during the raid at a house.

items.title: Over 7,000 turn up for Rain Rave day one, KL police chief warns public against misconduct
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The opening of the Rain Rave Water Music Festival (RRWMF), held in conjunction with Visit Malaysia Year 2026, saw a massive turnout with over 7,000 attendees gathered in the heart of the city. “Numerous roads are currently closed. Please pay close attention to official traffic notices and plan your journeys accordingly to avoid congestion.

items.title: Johor police chief: Two army personnel arrested over assault that left soldier with fractured skull
items.summary: MERSING, May 1 — Police have arrested two army personnel to assist in investigations into an incident that left a soldier seriously injured, including a fractured skull, at Kem Iskandar, here. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the two suspects, both 24 and from the same camp, were arrested by the Criminal Investigation Division of the Mersing district police headquarters at about 11.45 pm on March 14. Ab Rahaman said checks found that the suspects had no prior criminal records, and both tested negative for drugs.
